question_title: What will be Russia's annual GDP growth rate for Q2 2026?
summary: Q2 2026 Russia GDP annual (YoY) growth per Trading Economics. Q2 covers Apr-Jun 2026. Monthly GDP YoY: April +1.3%, May +0.3% (avg ~0.8%, decelerating); June unreleased. Quarterly YoY trajectory decelerated through 2025 (+1.4/+1.1/+0.6/+1.0) then hit -0.2% in Q1 2026 (first contraction since 2023). CBR describes Q2 as "moderate growth after the temporary decline at the start of the year." Full-year 2026 forecasts center 0.4-1.1% (Ministry 0.4, WB 0.8, CBR 0.5-1.5, IMF 1.1). A scenario mixture (60% base ~0.5%, 25% deeper-deceleration downside, 15% upside from base effects/monthly-quarterly divergence) yields median ~0.5%, P10 -0.05%, P90 ~1.08%, with ~12% probability of a negative print.

factors.description: Q2 2026 monthly GDP YoY April +1.3%, May +0.3% (avg ~0.8%), decelerating within quarter
CBR characterizes growth as 'moderate after temporary decline at start of year', implying positive Q2 rebound from Q1 -0.2%
Full-year 2026 forecasts center 0.4-1.1%; with Q1 at -0.2, later quarters must average higher
Sharp deceleration momentum (1.3->0.3 monthly) creates downside risk of near-flat or negative quarter

reasoning:
Forecast submitted for Russia's Q2 2026 annual GDP growth rate.

**Summary:** Median estimate **~0.5%**, with an 80% CI of roughly **-0.05% to +1.08%**.

**Key reasoning:**
- Q2 2026 covers April–June. Monthly GDP YoY came in at **+1.3% (April)** and **+0.3% (May)** — averaging ~0.8% but decelerating hard.
- The quarterly trajectory decelerated all through 2025 (+1.4/+1.1/+0.6/+1.0) then hit **-0.2% in Q1 2026**, the first contraction since 2023.
- The CBR describes Q2 as "moderate growth after the temporary decline at the beginning of the year," implying a weak positive rebound.
- Full-year 2026 forecasts center 0.4–1.1% (Ministry 0.4, World Bank 0.8, CBR 0.5–1.5, IMF 1.1).

I used a scenario mixture (60% base ~0.5%, 25% deeper-deceleration downside, 15% upside) to produce the distribution, placing ~12% probability on a negative print. The premortem reviewer approved, noting only that a marginal upward nudge to the center could be justified by the historical tendency of Russian quarterly figures to diverge upward from soft monthly reads.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as Russia's annual GDP growth rate for Q2 2026 according to [Trading Economics](https://tradingeconomics.com/russia/gdp-growth-annual) when checked by Metaculus on or after August 13, 2026.
fine_print: No other resolution source will be used in resolving this question.

factors.description: Q2 2026 monthly GDP YoY: April +1.3%, May +0.3% (avg ~0.8%), decelerating within quarter
CBR characterizes growth as 'moderate after temporary decline at start of year' - implies Q2 positive rebound from Q1 -0.2%
Full-year 2026 forecasts center 0.4-1.1% (Ministry 0.4, WB 0.8, CBR 0.5-1.5, IMF 1.1); with Q1 at -0.2, later quarters must average higher
Sharp deceleration momentum (1.3->0.3 monthly) creates downside risk that June/quarter comes in near flat or negative

input.anchor: Recent quarterly YoY trajectory + Q2 monthly average ~0.8% decelerating, forecaster full-year consensus ~0.7%
input.assessment: Q2 2026 covers Apr-Jun. Monthly GDP YoY: April +1.3%, May +0.3%, June unknown. Quarterly YoY sequence has decelerated: +1.4/+1.1/+0.6/+1.0 in 2025 then -0.2 in Q1 2026 (first contraction). CBR says growth resumed moderately after the temporary Q1 decline. Best estimate for Q2 quarterly YoY ~0.5%, reflecting the Apr-May average pulled slightly by an uncertain June and monthly-to-quarterly mapping noise. Scenario mixture (60% base ~0.5, 25% downside deeper deceleration, 15% upside) gives median 0.50, P10 -0.05, P90 1.08, P(<0)~12%.
input.key_uncertainties: June monthly figure (unknown), how Trading Economics quarterly figure maps from monthly data, and Russian data revisions.
input.tool_audit: research() returned excellent structured data with full quarterly sequence, monthly Q2 data, and all major forecaster projections cross-validated across Trading Economics, Rosstat, CBR, IMF, WB. execute_code used for Monte Carlo mixture. No failures.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was highly effective here, delivering exactly the time series and forecaster consensus needed. The main modeling judgment is mapping decelerating monthly YoY figures to a quarterly aggregate, which the scaffolding handles fine via code. No major friction.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified all four factors against the research trace and worldview store. No errors that would change the forecast.

GROUNDING CHECKS (all pass):
- Factor 1 (April +1.3%, May +0.3% monthly YoY): Confirmed in data_points and Trading Economics monthly-gdp-yoy snippet.
- Factor 2 (CBR "moderate growth after temporary decline at beginning of year"): Confirmed verbatim from cbr.ru June 19 2026 key-rate release.
- Factor 3 (Ministry 0.4, WB 0.8, CBR 0.5-1.5, IMF 1.1 full-year): All four confirmed in data_points with sources.
- Factor 4 (sector contractions manufacturing -1.5%, transport -1.8%, prof services -6.1%; deceleration momentum): Confirmed from Q1 2026 TE data.
No hallucinated evidence, no double-counting, no wrong-direction logits (all factors carry logit=0.0 and function as distribution-shaping inputs, consistent with their descriptions).

RESOLUTION ALIGNMENT: Resolution source is Trading Economics annual (YoY) GDP growth. The research used TE data directly and the quarterly YoY sequence is TE-sourced. The metric being modeled (quarterly YoY) matches the resolution metric. No misalignment. Resolution criteria were provided (not missing), so no recovery-fetch was required.

PRE-PUBLICATION: No factor claims Q2 is already resolved; Q2 data is genuinely unavailable as of publication (July 7), only April/May monthly reads exist. No pre-publication event problem.

PROBABILITY/DISTRIBUTION: The agent centers Q2 quarterly YoY at ~0.5% with P10 -0.05, P90 1.08, P(<0)~12%. My independent read: April/May monthly average is 0.8%, June uncertain; full-year consensus (0.4-1.1%) with Q1 at -0.2% implies remaining quarters average ~0.5-0.9%; CBR signals moderate resumption. A center of 0.5-0.6 with that spread is well-calibrated. I would arguably nudge the center marginally higher (~0.6) given the historical tendency for Russian quarterly YoY to diverge upward from weak monthly reads (e.g., Q4 2025 printed +1.0% despite soft monthly momentum), but this is inside the agent's range and not a meaningful disagreement. The agent explicitly acknowledged this upside-divergence in its counterargument.

SELF-EXAMINATION: The counterargument is substantive — it builds a coherent downside story (momentum collapse, deep sector contractions, 14.25% rate) and also flags live upside from monthly-to-quarterly divergence. Not a token gesture. Confidence 0.60 is appropriately humble given June is unreleased and no Q2 flash exists.

MINOR NOTE (not material): The tool_audit says "No failures," but ground-truth shows 1 error — the premortem tool call itself (the call that spawned this review), not any research tool. All research/search/execute_code calls succeeded. The discrepancy is trivial and does not affect any factor.
